title: RNA Isolation of Human Synovium and Fat Pad Tissues
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"70r09","text":"This protocol demonstrates how to perform RNA isolation from AllProtect preserved or fresh snapfrozen human synovium and fat pad tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Precool Benchtop Centrifuge and make sure temperature is at 4℃.
Prepare tools, mortar and pestle, and work surface by spraying down with RNAse Away.
Prepare piece of tissue, weigh out 120-150mg of tissue.
Add 3-4 ladles of liquid nitrogen to a clean mortar and pestle; allow to become cold.

Once the mortar and pestle are cold, add the snapfrozen tissue to the mortar and add a small amount of liquid nitrogen. Once the liquid nitrogen evaporates, start smashing and grinding the tissue quickly.

Add another ladle of liquid nitrogen to mortar and continue homogenizing the tissue. This is an ongoing process so keep adding liquid nitrogen until grinding creates a fine powder. Do not let the mortar and sample get warm.
Note - samples have a tendency to pop out of the mortar, having a silicone lid top on the mortar in the beginning helps keep tissue contained.

Once you have the desired consistency of a fine powder, add half a ladle of liquid nitrogen and tilt mortar slightly on its side and begin scraping liquid nitrogen/powdered tissue suspension to the bottom for a more thorough collection.
Just as the liquid nitrogen evaporates, use a pre-chilled scoop to transfer tissue powder right away to a chilled 2.0mL microtube with Trizol.
Use a benchtop vortex for 20 seconds max speed then place on ice for 1 minute. Repeat 2 times for a total of 3 times so that no clumps remain.
Place tube on Rotator at 4℃ and rotate for 20 minutes.
After 20 minutes, spin for 30 seconds in precooled benchtop centrifuge at 12,000g at 4℃
Transfer supernatant to pre-chilled 1.5mL microtube.
You may continue OR stop here and immediately place sample into a -80℃ freezer for next day RNA extraction.
Precool Benchtop Centrifuge to 4℃ AND make sure you have another benchtop centrifuge at Room Temperature.
Gradually thaw samples on wet ice if previously frozen at -80℃.
To each trizol tube: Add 111uL gDNA Eliminator, vortex for 15 seconds. Add 220uL Chloroform, vortex for 30 seconds, and set out at Room Temperature for 3 minutes.
Spin at 12,000g for 15 minutes in 4℃ benchtop centrifuge.
Transfer top aqueous layer to a Room Temperature 2.0mL microtube. Leave a generous layer behind and be careful to not disturb the middle and bottom layers.
Add 1:1 70% Ethanol to the tube with top aqueous layer. (For example- 600uL top layer and 600uL 70% Ethanol). Mix well by pipetting up and down.
Allow the top aqueous layer/ethanol mixture to come to Room Temperature.
Place 600uL into the Qiagen Column with the collection tube. When loading column, be careful to not get the rim wet. Do not load in more than 700uL of mixture to the column at a time.
Spin at 12,000 rpm in Room Temperature benchtop centrifuge.
Discard flow through after spin, invert collection tube and dab tube on paper towel to remove excess liquid.
Repeat steps 22, 23, and 24 until all of the mixture has gone through the column.
Place tubes in rack and open tops for 2 minutes to allow columns to air dry and remaining ethanol to evaporate. This improves 260/230 ratio.
Add 700uL of RWT buffer to each column.
Spin for 15 seconds at 12,000 rpm in Room Temperature centrifuge. Discard flow through.
Add 500uL of RPE buffer to each column, incubate at Room Temperature for 5 minutes.
Spin for 15 seconds at 12,000 rpm in Room Temperature centrifuge. Discard flow through.
Repeat steps 15 and 16 for two additional times, for a total of 3 RPE washes.
Spin for 2 minutes at 12,000 rpm in Room Temperature centrifuge.
Place column in new collection tube.
Spin-dry for 3 minutes at 12,000 rpm Room Temperature centrifuge.
Place column in new 1.5mL Eppendorf tube.
Add 30uL water to column to elute RNA.
Spin for 1 minute at 12,000 rpm in Room Temperature centrifuge
Place eluate back into column and spin once more for 1 minute at 12,000 rpm.
Check RNA concentration using Nanodrop.
Add 1 mL Trizol to Eppendorf tubes and place tubes on ice. For each Trizol-filled microtube, place another empty 2.0mL tube on ice.

AllProtect tissue: Thaw AllProtect samples at Room Temperature. Wash well in PBS in a petri dish, place onto kimwipe to remove liquid. Then weigh your tissue 120-150mg. Mince the tissue in a petri dish, then place in a 1.5mL tube and freeze in liquid nitrogen. This helps with the tissue homogenization steps.
Snapfrozen tissue: If you have to cut a smaller piece of snapfrozen tissue to fit weight range, do so in a petri dish placed on a piece of dry ice so sample remains frozen.
Perform the tissue grinding steps as quickly as possible to avoid any RNA degradation and tissue thawing.
creator: Merissa Olmer
